questions.question: Describe the main differences between 2PC (Two-Phase Commit) and 3PC (Three-Phase Commit) protocols. Explain how they handle failure scenarios in a distributed transaction.
questions.hint: Explain the protocols, their phases, and failure handling

questions.question: An application agent initiates a distributed transaction with a coordinator node. The coordinator node has two replicas, Node A and Node B, which are running different operating systems. What concurrency control technique should be used to prevent conflicts between concurrent transactions on Node A and Node B?

An application agent initiates a distributed transaction with a coordinator node. The coordinator node has two replicas, Node A and Node B. Node A is experiencing high CPU utilization, while Node B is experiencing high network latency. How can you prevent a distributed deadlock from occurring in this scenario?
questions.hint: Explain the strategies to prevent distributed deadlock
